QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_25

question.text: Which Act governs the setting up of depositories in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Depositories Act, 1996 enables the setting up of multiple depositories in India to ensure competition. Only a company registered under the Companies Act and sponsored by specified institutions can set up a depository. Two depositories exist: NSDL and CDSL. **The Trap:** Depositories must obtain both a certificate of registration AND a certificate of commencement of business from SEBI before starting operations. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VI (Depository Operations) covers the Depositories Act extensively. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Depositories Act 1996 framework: NSDL (1996) and CDSL (1999). Key principle: depositary cannot directly open client accounts — must work through Depository Participants (DPs). Rights and obligations of all stakeholders are clearly defined.
